mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: inguinal hernia, abnormal lymph node morphology, carcinoid tumor
renal involvement (hypertension), angiokeratomas (perianal dermatitis), gastrointestinal symptoms (abnormal bowel sounds)
inguinal hernia, cafe au lait spots (not mentioned but common feature), lymphadenopathy
benign hamartomatous polyps of the intestine and skin (not explicitly stated but suggested by "abnormal rectum morphology" and perianal dermatitis), increased risk of thyroid cancer
hypertension, benign growths or malformations in multiple organs including brain, kidneys, heart, eyes, and skin
hepatosplenomegaly (may cause abdominal pain leading to incorrect diagnosis as hernia), neurological signs such as tremors, peripheral neuropathies, and psychiatric disturbances
abdominal cramps, diarrhea, constipation, and other gastrointestinal issues due to autonomic dysfunction
recurrent fever episodes with serositis, erysipelas-like cutaneous lesions, arthralgias, and allergic reactions
telangiectases (mucocutaneous lesions may be present around the anal region), gastrointestinal bleeding causing intermittent abdominal pain
scoliosis (can lead to inguinal hernias secondary to spinal deformation), optic nerve damage resulting in visual impairment
